CARROT AND GINGER SOUP

This warming soup has been a staple in my kitchen for years, especially during the
colder months when fresh ginger is abundant at the market. The natural sweetness of
the carrots combines beautifully with the sharp, bright notes of ginger and a whisper
of orange zest. I often make this in large batches and freeze portions for quick
weeknight dinners. It's equally delicious served hot or chilled as a summer appetizer.

Ingredients:
- 2 lbs carrots, peeled and chopped
- 1 medium onion, diced
- 3-inch piece fresh ginger, peeled and sliced
- 4 cups vegetable stock (or chicken stock)
- ½ cup heavy cream (or coconut milk)
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- Zest and juice of 1 orange
- Salt and white pepper to taste
- Fresh cilantro for garnish
- Toasted caraway seeds (optional)

Preparation:
1. Heat oil in large pot over medium heat
2. Sauté onion until translucent, about 4-5 minutes
3. Add carrot pieces and ginger slices
4. Cook 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally
5. Pour in stock and bring to simmer
6. Cook 25-30 minutes until carrots very tender
7. Remove ginger slices
8. Blend soup in batches until smooth (or use immersion blender)
9. Return to pot and stir in cream
10. Season with salt, pepper, orange zest and juice
11. Simmer gently for 5 minutes
12. Serve hot with cilantro and caraway seeds
